input.description: Check plan file and evidence dir

input.content:
Bead ddx-86ccbb75 is an epic whose acceptance criterion is "All child beads closed." It carries no implementation work of its own; the children carry the testable ACs.

Children (queried via `ddx bead list --json | jq '.[] | select(.parent=="ddx-86ccbb75")'`):
- ddx-0e6ff1c5 [open] doc-graph: profile current layout — label widths, settle time, degree distribution
- ddx-7a26141e [open] doc-graph: Playwright test for non-overlapping layout + no regression
- ddx-a9edd569 [open] doc-graph: label-aware collision, degree-tuned forces, bounded convergence, settle/freeze semantics

What is done: nothing in this attempt; the epic is a tracking node.

What is blocking: all three children are still open. The epic cannot be closed until each child lands its own implementation/tests and closes. Executing the epic directly would conflate three independent slices (profiling, implementation, e2e test) into one attempt — exactly the size-check failure mode called out in the execute-bead instructions.

What a follow-up attempt would need: the queue should drain the three child beads first. Once all three are closed, this epic can be closed administratively (no code change required) — its AC is satisfied by child closure, not by edits in this worktree.

No commit produced; no decomposition needed (children already exist).

data.text: Epic bead with three open children and no implementation of its own. Wrote no_changes_rationale.txt documenting that the epic is gated on child closure (ddx-0e6ff1c5, ddx-7a26141e, ddx-a9edd569) — those need to drain first before this epic can close administratively. No commit.
